plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure and remove positions below stop.
Trim SPY to 20% to cure concentration flag and comply with mandate.
Reduce QQQI to 10% due to its downtrend and to reduce overall exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
Increase QQQ to 25% to maintain broad market exposure with a strong uptrend.
This creates a cash buffer of approximately 15-20% for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expected swings will be much smaller, but so will potential upside.

plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate positions below stop and reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration flag and comply with mandate.
Reduce QQQI to 15% due to its downtrend and to rebalance the portfolio.
Buy ANET at 5% to add a high risk/reward quality tech name.
Buy TXN at 5% to add a quality semiconductor name with good R/R.
This plan maintains a cash buffer of approximately 10% for risk-first growth.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality tech names. Expect moderate swings, balancing growth with drawdown control.

plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop and reallocate capital.
Trim SPY to 25% to cure concentration flag and comply with mandate.
Sell all QQQI to exit a downtrending income ETF, freeing up capital.
Buy NOW at 10% as it has the highest scanner score and strong uptrend.
Buy NVDU at 10% for its strong risk/reward and growth potential.
Buy TECS at 5% as a leveraged short tech position to hedge against concentration.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It emphasizes high-scoring setups and uses leveraged positions, which amplifies both upside and drawdown. Be aware of daily decay in leveraged ETFs and strict stop discipline is critical to manage significant potential losses.

plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETFs and positions below stop.
Sell all SMH and CRWD to exit downtrending sector and high-beta single names.
Increase SPY to 35% for broad market exposure and stability.
Increase QQQI to 30% to maximize income-focused ETF exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
A significant cash buffer will be held, approximately 15-20%.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes steady distributions and low drawdown, cutting high-beta and leveraged positions. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
